title: Oven Temperature Sensor Open Circuit
shortDescription: The oven cavity temperature sensor circuit is open.
fullDescription: Error E003 on Thermador ovens indicates the oven temperature sensor (RTD probe) has an open circuit. Thermador is a BSH (Bosch-Siemens) brand, so error code formats follow BSH conventions. The oven cannot regulate heating without a valid sensor reading.

causes.cause: Sensor connector is loose or corroded
Sensor wiring is broken near the oven cavity
Control board sensor input has failed

fixSteps.title: Check sensor connections
fixSteps.instruction: Access the rear panel. Locate the temperature sensor connector and ensure it is firmly seated.

fixSteps.title: Test sensor resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect sensor and measure. At room temperature an RTD reads approximately 1080-1100 ohms. OL means open.

fixSteps.title: Inspect sensor wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Trace wires from connector to oven cavity. Look for heat-damaged insulation or breaks.

fixSteps.title: Replace the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove mounting screw inside the oven, pull out old sensor, feed in new one, reconnect.

whenToCallPro: If a new sensor does not clear E003, the control board needs professional diagnosis.
videoSearchQuery: Thermador oven E003 temperature sensor error fix

faq.q: Is the Thermador sensor the same as Bosch?
faq.a: Yes. Thermador and Bosch are both BSH brands and share many components including oven temperature sensors.

faq.q: Can I use the oven with E003?
faq.a: No. Without a working temperature sensor, the oven cannot safely regulate temperature.

faq.q: Is the sensor easy to replace?
faq.a: Yes, it is one of the simplest oven repairs — one screw inside the oven and a plug at the back.

title: Temperature Sensor Shorted
shortDescription: The oven temperature sensor is reading a shorted condition.
fullDescription: Error E005 indicates the sensor circuit has near-zero resistance (shorted). The control interprets this as an impossibly high temperature and shuts down for safety.

causes.cause: Sensor wiring pinched creating a short

fixSteps.title: Test sensor resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect sensor. Should read 1080-1100 ohms at room temp. Near zero means shorted.

fixSteps.title: Check for moisture
fixSteps.instruction: Dry the connector with compressed air.

fixSteps.title: Check wiring for shorts
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect for pinched wires where bare conductors touch.

fixSteps.title: Replace the sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove screw, pull out old, install new.

whenToCallPro: If new sensor does not fix E005.
videoSearchQuery: Thermador oven E005 sensor shorted error fix

faq.q: Can steam cause E005?
faq.a: Yes. Steam condensation can short the connector temporarily.

faq.q: Is E005 the same fix as E003?
faq.a: Same sensor, same procedure. E003 is open, E005 is shorted.

faq.q: Are Thermador parts expensive?
faq.a: Thermador sensors are reasonably priced at $20-$50, similar to Bosch.

title: Oven Over-Temperature
shortDescription: The oven has exceeded the maximum safe temperature limit.
fullDescription: Error E011 indicates the oven cavity has exceeded its safety threshold. The control shuts down heating. This is a critical safety code.

fixSteps.title: Cool the oven
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off and open door. Wait 30-60 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Test sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Should read 1080-1100 ohms at room temp.

fixSteps.title: Replace sensor if faulty
fixSteps.instruction: Standard sensor replacement procedure.

fixSteps.title: Check for stuck relay
fixSteps.instruction: If sensor is good, board relay is stuck. Board replacement needed.

whenToCallPro: If sensor is good, stuck relay is a fire hazard. Call immediately.
videoSearchQuery: Thermador oven E011 over temperature error fix

faq.q: Is E011 dangerous?
faq.a: Yes. Disconnect power until repaired.

faq.q: Can self-clean trigger E011?
faq.a: Yes, self-clean temperatures can trigger E011 if the sensor has drifted.

faq.q: Does Thermador recommend self-clean?
faq.a: Thermador recommends using self-clean sparingly and preferring manual cleaning.

title: Door Lock Failure
shortDescription: The oven door lock mechanism has failed.
fullDescription: Error E030 indicates the door lock motor or switch is not functioning correctly. Self-clean cannot proceed without confirmed door lock.

fixSteps.title: Power reset
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off breaker for 5 minutes to free stuck door.

fixSteps.title: Clean latch
fixSteps.instruction: Remove debris from latch at top of door opening.

fixSteps.title: Test lock switches
fixSteps.instruction: Test switch continuity in both positions.

fixSteps.title: Replace latch assembly
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power. Access and replace the latch.

whenToCallPro: If door is stuck locked after power reset.
videoSearchQuery: Thermador oven E030 door lock error fix

faq.q: Can I still bake?
faq.a: Usually yes. E030 only affects self-clean.

faq.q: How do I unlock a stuck door?
faq.a: Power reset usually works. If not, look for manual release.

faq.q: Is the latch the same as Bosch?
faq.a: Many Thermador latch parts are shared with Bosch. Verify the part number.

title: Control Board Communication Error
shortDescription: Communication failure between the control board and user interface.
fullDescription: Error E035 indicates the main control and user interface boards cannot communicate. The serial data link is broken.
symptoms: Oven may run on last setting

fixSteps.title: Power cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off breaker for 5 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Reseat cable
fixSteps.instruction: Access both boards and firmly reseat connecting cable.

fixSteps.title: Inspect for damage
fixSteps.instruction: Check cable and connectors for heat or corrosion damage.

fixSteps.title: Replace faulty board
fixSteps.instruction: Professional diagnosis determines which board.

whenToCallPro: If reseating cables does not fix E035.
videoSearchQuery: Thermador oven E035 communication error fix

faq.q: Is it safe to use with intermittent E035?
faq.a: No. Loss of communication during operation is unsafe.

faq.q: Which board fails more often?
faq.a: Both fail at similar rates. Testing identifies which.

faq.q: Are Thermador boards expensive?
faq.a: Yes. $200-$500, reflecting premium quality.

title: Control Board EEPROM Error
shortDescription: The control board configuration memory is corrupted.
fullDescription: Error E036 indicates the EEPROM data on the control board is unreadable or corrupted. The board cannot operate without valid calibration data.

fixSteps.title: Extended power cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off breaker for 10 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Verify board
fixSteps.instruction: Check part number matches your model.

fixSteps.title: Check connections
fixSteps.instruction: Reseat all board connectors.

fixSteps.title: Replace the board
fixSteps.instruction: E036 requires board replacement.

whenToCallPro: E036 almost always requires board replacement.
videoSearchQuery: Thermador oven E036 EEPROM error fix

faq.q: Can EEPROM be reprogrammed?
faq.a: Not in the field. Board replacement is required.

faq.q: What causes corruption?
faq.a: Power surges or interruptions during write operations.

faq.q: Is E036 covered by warranty?
faq.a: If within warranty period, a defective board should be covered.

title: Convection Fan Motor Failure
shortDescription: The convection fan motor is not operating at expected speed.
fullDescription: Error E040 indicates the convection fan has stalled, runs too slowly, or did not start. Thermador ovens rely heavily on convection for their cooking performance.

fixSteps.title: Check for obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Remove racks and look at fan for debris.

fixSteps.title: Test motor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect and test resistance. Spin blade by hand.

fixSteps.title: Check connections
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure motor connector is seated. Look for heat damage.

fixSteps.title: Replace motor
fixSteps.instruction: Remove rear oven panel. Unbolt and replace motor.

whenToCallPro: If motor tests fine but E040 persists.
videoSearchQuery: Thermador oven E040 convection fan motor error fix

faq.q: Can I use bake without convection?
faq.a: Some models allow standard bake with E040.

faq.q: How long do motors last?
faq.a: 8-15 years under normal use.

faq.q: Is the Thermador motor the same as Bosch?
faq.a: Some are shared. Verify the part number for your model.

title: Cooling Fan Failure
shortDescription: The oven cooling fan is not running.
fullDescription: Error E045 indicates the cooling fan that protects cabinetry and electronics from oven heat is not operating. Critical for built-in installations.

fixSteps.title: Check for blockage
fixSteps.instruction: Access cooling fan area. Clear any debris.

fixSteps.title: Test the motor
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect and test resistance.

fixSteps.title: Check wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect for heat damage.

fixSteps.title: Replace the fan
fixSteps.instruction: Disconnect power, remove and replace the motor.

whenToCallPro: Do not use the oven without a working cooling fan.
videoSearchQuery: Thermador oven E045 cooling fan error fix

faq.q: Is it safe without the cooling fan?
faq.a: No. It protects cabinets and electronics from heat damage.

faq.q: Why does the fan run after cooking?
faq.a: Normal. It dissipates residual heat.

faq.q: How long does it run?
faq.a: 30-60 minutes after the oven is turned off.

title: Meat Probe Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The meat probe temperature sensor is reading out of range.
fullDescription: Error E050 indicates the meat probe circuit has an open or shorted condition. The probe may be defective, or the probe jack has a fault.

causes.cause: Probe jack is dirty or corroded

symptoms: Oven may not start with probe in

fixSteps.title: Remove and test probe
fixSteps.instruction: Unplug probe. If E050 clears, probe is faulty. Test resistance.

fixSteps.title: Clean probe jack
fixSteps.instruction: Clean the jack inside the oven with a cotton swab.

fixSteps.title: Inspect probe cable
fixSteps.instruction: Check cable for damage, especially near the plug.

fixSteps.title: Replace the probe
fixSteps.instruction: Order the correct Thermador meat probe for your model.

whenToCallPro: If new probe and clean jack do not fix E050.
videoSearchQuery: Thermador oven E050 meat probe error fix

faq.q: Can I use the oven without the probe?
faq.a: Yes. Unplug the probe and use standard temperature cooking.

faq.q: Can I use a Bosch probe?
faq.a: Some Bosch probes are compatible. Verify the part number.

faq.q: How do I clean the probe?
faq.a: Wash with warm soapy water. Do not submerge the plug end.

title: Power Supply Voltage Error
shortDescription: The oven is not receiving correct supply voltage.
fullDescription: Error E060 indicates incorrect supply voltage. Thermador ovens require a dedicated 240V circuit with proper amperage.

fixSteps.title: Reset breaker
fixSteps.instruction: Turn breaker fully off and back on.

fixSteps.title: Test voltage
fixSteps.instruction: Measure at junction box: 240V between hot legs.

fixSteps.title: Check terminals
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure all wire connections are tight.

fixSteps.title: Call electrician
fixSteps.instruction: If voltage is incorrect at the panel.

whenToCallPro: If voltage is incorrect, call an electrician.
videoSearchQuery: Thermador oven E060 voltage error 240V fix

faq.q: Does Thermador need a dedicated circuit?
faq.a: Yes. A dedicated 240V circuit with proper amperage rating.

faq.q: Can a surge protector help?
faq.a: A whole-house surge protector at the panel can prevent surge damage.

faq.q: Will E060 damage the oven?
faq.a: E060 protects the oven by preventing operation on incorrect voltage.

title: Bake Element Failure
shortDescription: The bake heating element is not drawing expected current.
fullDescription: Error E070 indicates the bake element is open (burned out) or its wiring is disconnected. The oven cannot bake without a functioning bake element.

fixSteps.title: Inspect element
fixSteps.instruction: Look for breaks, blisters, or holes in the bake element.

fixSteps.title: Test resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Should be 20-50 ohms. OL means open.

fixSteps.title: Check wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect wiring from element to board.

fixSteps.title: Replace element
fixSteps.instruction: Remove mounting screws, disconnect wires, install replacement.

whenToCallPro: If element tests good but E070 persists.
videoSearchQuery: Thermador oven E070 bake element error fix

faq.q: How long do Thermador elements last?
faq.a: 5-15 years depending on usage.

faq.q: Can I see when it fails?
faq.a: Sometimes a visible break or hole, but not always.

faq.q: Are Thermador elements shared with Bosch?
faq.a: Some are. Verify the exact part number.

title: Broil Element Failure
shortDescription: The broil heating element is not drawing expected current.
fullDescription: Error E080 indicates the broil element at the top of the oven cavity has failed or its circuit is open. Broiling and certain baking modes will not function.
symptoms: No browning on top of food

fixSteps.title: Inspect element
fixSteps.instruction: Look at the broil element at the top of the oven for visible damage.

fixSteps.title: Test resistance
fixSteps.instruction: Should be 15-40 ohms. OL means open.

fixSteps.title: Check wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect connections at the element terminals.

fixSteps.title: Replace element
fixSteps.instruction: Remove mounting hardware, disconnect wires, install replacement.

whenToCallPro: If element tests fine but E080 persists.
videoSearchQuery: Thermador oven E080 broil element error fix

faq.q: Can I still bake with E080?
faq.a: Yes, standard bake mode uses only the bake element and should still work.

faq.q: Is the broil element different from bake?
faq.a: Yes. Different shape, location, and typically different resistance values.

faq.q: Can I replace the broil element myself?
faq.a: Yes, it is a straightforward DIY repair with basic tools.
